refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period using telemetry logs and compare against the customer-defined availability threshold.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or mitigate link failures to maintain availability.
FR4: Ensure link availability meets or exceeds customer-defined target.

candidate_dps: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover
Real-time link health monitoring and alerting system
Adaptive bandwidth management to prioritize teleoperation traffic
Robust error correction and retransmission protocols
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover

potential_sources: Interaction with Safety Services for failover alerts
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability management via redundancy, minimizing coupling with other system functions except necessary safety alerts.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover mechanisms.
Risk of increased complexity leading to potential integration issues.
High safety criticality demands rigorous validation of failover logic.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring DP as a complementary function to enhance detection and response.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation communication links, including redundancy and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections used for teleoperation data transmission and redundancy management.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send link status and health data to monitoring and logging systems.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Interface to notify Safety Services of link failures or degraded availability impacting safety.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of link availability logic.
Vehicle module has limited direct control over teleoperation link redundancy.
Highest containment score; centralizes link availability management and minimizes diffusion.
Primarily receives alerts; not responsible for link management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes link availability management, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Teleoperation module responsibilities if monitoring and recovery functions are not well modularized.
Interface coordination with Safety Services must be clearly defined to avoid delays in safety alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear submodules within Teleoperation for monitoring, redundancy control, and failover to manage complexity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant communication channels with automatic failover) directly addresses the refined FR by providing a logical solution to maintain link availability.

diffusion_risks: Risk of complexity within Teleoperation module if multiple DPs are combined without clear boundaries.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment of the DP with minimal diffusion to other modules, supporting maintainability and ownership clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and safety alert logs is feasible within the Teleoperation module scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module aligns with system architecture and stakeholder ownership.

recommended_revisions: Consider adding complementary DPs for monitoring and adaptive management to enhance robustness.
Define submodule boundaries within Teleoperation to manage complexity and interface clarity.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, complementary DPs and clearer submodule definitions within Teleoperation are needed to fully address complexity and safety criticality.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or mitigate link failures to maintain availability.
FR4: Ensure link availability meets or exceeds customer-defined target.

revise.why: To incorporate additional DPs for monitoring and adaptive management that complement the selected redundancy DP.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces are reliable and timely.
open_risks: Potential delays in safety alert propagation.
evidence_needed: Performance data from redundant link implementations.
Verification results from telemetry and safety alert systems.
